title: caltha arvensis
intro: Caltha arvensis, also known as marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its bright yellow and orange flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of caltha arvensis include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Caltha arvensis is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion, and can also be used in herbal teas, salves, and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
